Two new reports were published during 2 CHAIRMAN’S STATEMENT SHARE BUYBACK Over the course of 2024, the Group maintained its share-buyback programme and, by the end of the year, had deployed US$13.4 million (2023 — US$9.7 million) to purchase, and subsequently cancel, 1,183,398 (2023 — 991,198) of the Company’s 10p shares. This represented Operational highlights 2.2% (2023 — 1.8%) of the issued share capital. Evans is a responsible producer of sustainable Indonesian palm oil, striving for excellence in all the Group’s operations, with a focus on continuing growth and offering an increasing yield. 1 M.P. EVANS GROUP PLC ANNUAL REPORT 2024 Chairman’s statement 2024 and are available on the Group’s website, setting out in detail our approach to carbon reduction and on broader environmental, social and governance (“ESG”) matters. In this annual report, we provide an updated 2024 carbon balance sheet, demonstrating that we are on track, for both the interim and long term, to achieve our overall carbon-reduction target. We are reviewed by the Zoological Society of London for the quality of our disclosures on ESG, and received a further increase in their 2024 ‘SPOTT’ rating, up to 88.9%. The Group achieved a gross profit of The Group operated six palm-oil US$116.6 million in 2024, almost 50% mills throughout 2024 with total crop higher than in the previous year, and an processed of 1.6 million tonnes. all-time record. A combination of efficient operations and strong pricing resulted in record Earnings per share were 129.6p, significantly higher than profitability, and earnings per share the 78.1p recorded in 2023. The high-price environment of 129.6p. benefited the Group during the year, most notably in the second half, along with lower operating costs, including Peter Hadsley-Chaplin from a continued reduction in fertiliser costs. The Group Chairman generated a large amount of operating cash, with net operating cash inflows of US$135.8 million (2023 — US$79.7 million). This was put to good use in returning funds to shareholders, continued capital investment, and RESULTS eliminating Group net debt. At the end of the year, the The Group achieved a gross profit of US$116.6 million in
